clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Determine when to initiate antihypertensive medications based on BP level and cardiovascular risk factors
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Initial HTN diagnosis, elevated BP on repeat measurement, treatment decision point
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Medication initiation decision (YES/NO) with specific BP thresholds based on risk category

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for treatment initiation decision
Show calculated average BP from previous measurements
Ask about existing CHD, stroke, or HF
Check diabetes, CKD, or calculate PREVENT-CVD risk

validationRules: Average BP should be calculated from ≥2 readings on ≥2 occasions
PREVENT-CVD risk calculated using validated calculator
CKD defined as eGFR <60 or UACR ≥30 for ≥3 months

rules: Step 1: Check if average BP ≥140/90 → if YES, initiate medications (primary/secondary prevention not specified)
Step 2: If BP <140/90, check for existing clinical CVD (CHD, stroke, HF) → if YES, initiate if SBP ≥130 OR DBP ≥80 (secondary prevention)
Step 3: If no clinical CVD, check for diabetes OR CKD OR PREVENT-CVD risk ≥7.5% → if YES, initiate if SBP ≥130 OR DBP ≥80 (primary prevention)
Step 4: If none of above met → do not initiate medications at this time, proceed to other management
Prevention type: Secondary = existing CVD; Primary = no CVD but has risk factors

decisionTree.condition: Existing clinical CVD (CHD, stroke, HF) AND average SBP ≥130 OR DBP ≥80
Diabetes OR CKD OR PREVENT-CVD risk ≥7.5% AND average SBP ≥130 OR DBP ≥80

decisionTree.action: Do not initiate medications; proceed to other management

rationale.description: Explanation of which criteria triggered treatment

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: If treatment initiated: Start first-line antihypertensive (thiazide, ACEi, ARB, CCB). If not initiated: Lifestyle modification, repeat BP in 3-6 months, or evaluate for other management
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: If treatment started: Follow up in 4 weeks for BP and side effects. If not started: Reassess in 3-6 months or sooner if BP trends up
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Confirm elevated BP with home or ambulatory monitoring before treatment initiation
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag patients with BP ≥160/100 for more urgent follow-up; flag PREVENT-CVD risk ≥20% for aggressive risk factor modification
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log decision pathway with BP values, risk factors, and treatment initiation status
